channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon rocket arrives back at the launch pad - Space
item.description:
1. NASA's Artemis 2 moon rocket arrives back at the launch pad  Space
  2. Here's how you can see NASA's SLS rocket before the Artemis II launch  Florida Today
  3. NASA rolls Artemis II moon rocket back to its launch pad for second time; high winds delayed rollout time  Spectrum News 13
  4. NASA Finalizes Artemis II Rollout, Crew Begins Quarantine  NASA (.gov)
  5. How the Artemis moon missions are shaping the next era of exploration  PBS

item.description:
1. Meteorite hunters scour Ohio for fragments of 7-ton space rock that crashed into Earth  Phys.org

  4. Moment suspected meteor is spotted over Ohio and Pennsylvania  BBC
  5. Meteor fragments in Medina County? See the list of 14 places where meteorites have been found before in Ohio  WKYC

item.description:

  4. Comet 3I/ATLAS and comet SWAN seen from NASA's PUNCH  MSN

item.title: We keep finding the raw material of DNA in asteroids—what’s it telling us? - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. We keep finding the raw material of DNA in asteroids—what’s it telling us?  Ars Technica

  3. Ryugu asteroid samples contain all DNA and RNA building blocks, bolstering origin-of-life theories  Phys.org
  4. DNA building blocks on asteroid Ryugu, bacteria that eat plastic waste, and more science news  Engadget
  5. The asteroid Ryugu has all of the main ingredients for life  New Scientist

item.title: Opening a new window into superconductivity by reimagining a classic tool - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Opening a new window into superconductivity by reimagining a classic tool  Phys.org

  4. Nanoscale ridges in a substrate add 15 K and 50 Tesla to a superconductor’s limits  R&D World
